headline: Why U.S. Stocks Aren't Crashing With Iran War
summary: A major U.S. strike on Iran has ignited an open war, immediately threatening the Strait of Hormuz - the vital chokepoint for roughly a fifth of the world's oil. Historically, a sudden shock to global energy supplies of this magnitude is the textbook trigger for a severe market downturn. During the 1973 Arab Oil Embargo, surging energy prices led to stagflation that wiped out approximately 48% of the S&P 500's value.
